rationale_for_baseline_tier: Bilateral allergic conjunctivitis with preserved vision and no red-eye danger signs or anaphylaxis is benign and manageable at home with topical antihistamine and allergen avoidance, GP/telehealth review if not settling (T1). Dramatic swelling does not change the tier once emergencies are excluded, but red-eye and anaphylaxis carry hard T4/T5 safety-nets.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Loss or blurring of vision, severe deep eye pain, pain on moving the eye, marked light sensitivity, a bulging eye, or it becoming one-sided and painful
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same day — urgent eye care / ED
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests keratitis, uveitis, glaucoma or orbital cellulitis — sight-threatening.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your vision drops, you get severe deep eye pain, pain moving your eye, strong light sensitivity, or one eye becomes painful and bulging — get urgent eye care or go to ED that day.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Swelling of lips/tongue/throat, difficulty breathing or swallowing
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your lips, tongue or throat swell or you have any trouble breathing — call 000 immediately.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone; transient visual restriction from lid swelling
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Provide clear red-eye red flags; advise keeping a phone to hand while vision is limited.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: You can manage this at home: antihistamine eye drops, cold compresses, don't rub, and avoid the allergen. It should improve within hours to a few days.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Self-care with telehealth/GP review; urgent eye care/ED for red-eye danger signs; 000 for anaphylaxis
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Urgent eye care or ED at any time for vision loss or severe eye pain; 000 for airway swelling.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Bilateral acute allergic conjunctivitis; red-eye and anaphylaxis screens negative; topical antihistamine + avoidance.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without confirming vision or giving a red-eye safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A concurrent keratitis/uveitis is dismissed as allergy and vision is permanently damaged.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No anaphylaxis safety-net despite the allergic mechanism.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Emergency referral/ambulance for benign bilateral allergic conjunctivitis with preserved vision — unnecessary.
